caption: Step 1: Open eBay Advanced Search
narration: And... action. Let's start by opening eBay Advanced Search. Every good walkthrough needs an establishing shot, and this is ours.

caption: Step 2: Enter main search keywords
narration: Now the camera swings to the next beat. We enter main search keywords. This is the boring part, which makes it perfect for automation.

caption: Step 3: Change the keyword match dropdown to exact order
description: Change the keyword match dropdown to exact order
narration: Here comes the payoff shot. We change the keyword match dropdown to exact order. It keeps the flow easy to follow without sounding like a checklist.

caption: Step 4: Enter words to exclude from results
description: Enter words to exclude from results
narration: Quick scene change. We enter words to exclude from results. Nobody wins an award for manual retyping, so the automation can have this one.

caption: Step 5: Include title and description in the search scope
description: Include title and description in the search scope
narration: Cue the montage. We include title and description in the search scope. It keeps the flow easy to follow without sounding like a checklist.

caption: Step 6: Set minimum and maximum price filters
description: Set minimum and maximum price filters
narration: Now the camera swings to the next beat. We set minimum and maximum price filters. Being explicit here keeps reruns consistent.

caption: Step 7: Filter for new-condition items
narration: Here comes the payoff shot. We filter for new-condition items. It keeps the flow easy to follow without sounding like a checklist.

caption: Step 8: Run the search and open the filtered results page
description: Run the search and open the filtered results page
narration: And that is our closing scene. We run the search and open the filtered results page. This is the button where all the setup finally pays off.
